title: leptorumohra miqueliana subsp. fimbriata
intro: Leptorumohra miqueliana subsp. fimbriata is a plant species within the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as the fern family. It is a type of fern that is native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in the Philippines and Indonesia. Leptorumohra miqueliana subsp. fimbriata has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin infections, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and poultices for medicinal use.
